score.cold_response_classification_justificati: The model confidently and accurately describes the Transformer architecture without any uncertainty signals, matching canonical content.
per_dimension_justifications: Cold and post responses both clearly distinguish Transformer from RNN via parallel self-attention vs sequential recurrent hidden state, matching canonical features.
Cold and post responses cleanly separate Transformer from CNN by contrasting global self-attention with local convolution and hierarchical receptive fields.
Both responses correctly identify that MoE is a modification to the Transformer FFN sublayer rather than a competing architecture, naming sparse vs dense activation as the distinguishing feature.

score.cold_response_classification_justificati: The cold response confidently recognized the established transformer architecture and gave canonical features: self-attention, multi-head attention, feed-forward sublayers, positional encodings, encoder/decoder variants, and parallel sequence processing rather than recurrence. The available labels do not include a separate 'correct recognition' category, so this is coded as recognition rather than uncertainty, refusal, or confabulation.
per_dimension_justifications: RNN: Cold and post responses cleanly distinguish transformer parallel self-attention from recurrent/sequential hidden-state processing, including long-range dependency and parallelization differences.
CNN: Cold and post responses cleanly distinguish transformer global self-attention over tokens from CNN convolutional local receptive fields, spatial locality, and hierarchical feature extraction.

MoE: Cold and post responses cleanly distinguish transformer as the base self-attention architecture from MoE as a sparse routing/expert technique often replacing or modifying FFN sublayers inside transformer blocks.

score.cold_response_classification_justificati: The model provides a detailed, accurate, and comprehensive explanation of the Transformer architecture, demonstrating full recognition of the established technical term. It correctly identifies the core mechanism (self-attention), the problem it solved (RNN limitations), and its key components.
per_dimension_justifications: Cold Distinguishability: The model perfectly distinguishes the Transformer from all three near-neighbors. vs. RNN: It correctly identifies the core difference as parallel processing with self-attention versus sequential processing with recurrence. vs. CNN: It correctly identifies the difference as global, relational context versus local, hierarchical pattern detection via convolution. vs. MoE: It correctly and sophisticatedly identifies MoE not as an alternative architecture, but as a modification to the Transformer's FFN layer to enable sparse computation.

Post-Introduction Distinguishability: After receiving the definition, the model's performance remains perfect. It reiterates and refines its distinctions, often structuring them in tables. It correctly contrasts Transformer's parallel processing and self-attention with RNN's sequential recurrence, its global attention with CNN's local convolution, and its dense FFN layers with the sparse, conditional FFNs in an MoE setup.

Confabulation Severity: The model showed perfect recognition of the established term 'transformer (architecture)' from the start. It provided a correct, detailed definition without any uncertainty or error, so there was no confabulation.

Refusal of Collapse: The model explicitly and correctly distinguished the Transformer from all three near-neighbors after the definition was provided. It named non-trivial features for each: parallel processing via self-attention (vs. RNN's recurrence), global relational attention (vs. CNN's local convolution), and the use of dense FFN layers (vs. MoE's sparse expert layers).
